settings.title: Welcoming Dr Owusu to the Practice
settings.excerpt: Our new vet brings a special interest in older pets and the gentle care they need.
settings.body:
We are delighted to introduce Dr Owusu, who has joined the team at Tailside this month. With more vets on hand, you should find it easier to get an appointment that suits you.

Dr Owusu has a particular interest in senior pets, from managing arthritis to spotting the subtle signs that an older cat or dog is uncomfortable. If your companion is getting on in years, she is a wonderful person to see.

A focus on older animals

Caring for a senior pet is its own kind of work. The changes are gradual, the signs are quiet, and good care is often about small adjustments rather than big interventions. Dr Owusu enjoys exactly this part of the job, and she is patient with the slow detective work it sometimes takes.

She is also calm and unhurried with anxious animals, which makes a real difference for nervous patients.

When it might be worth a visit

If your pet is getting on in years, a few signs are worth a check up:

  • Stiffness after rest, or hesitation on the stairs.
  • Changes in drinking, appetite, or weight.
  • Sleeping more, or seeming less interested in usual things.

You can request Dr Owusu by name when you book. We are so pleased to have her with us.
